title: tetracera sarmentosa subsp. sumatrana
intro: Tetracera sarmentosa subsp. sumatrana, also known as Sumatran snake vine, is a plant species belonging to the family Erebidae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and providing relief from pain and swelling. Herbal preparations of Tetracera sarmentosa subsp. sumatrana are typically made by boiling the leaves or roots in water, and are used as teas or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
